put.description: Updates an existing Excel/JSON datasource collection by its ID. Requires admin role.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the Excel datasource to update

schema.description: Updated Excel sheet data and configuration

post.description: Checks whether an Excel/JSON datasource with the given name already exists in the system. Requires admin role.

name.description: Name of the Excel datasource to check for existence

200.description: Returns boolean indicating whether the datasource exists.

500.description: Server error checking Excel datasource existence.

post.description: Sends a natural language query to the AI assistant and returns a response with data or chart suggestions.

query.description: The natural language question to send to the AI assistant

datasource.description: The datasource ID to query against

500.description: Server error processing the AI request.

get.description: Checks if the AI assistant is available and configured with a valid API key.

200.description: Returns availability status of the AI assistant.

post.description: Sends a custom prompt to the AI assistant for advanced query generation.

datasource.description: The datasource ID to use as context

post.description: Requests AI-generated chart type suggestions based on the current query and data context.

query.description: Current query context for chart suggestion

data.description: Sample data to base suggestions on

get.description: Retrieves the current AI assistant configuration (model, API key status, availability).

post.description: Saves the AI assistant configuration including API key, model selection and other settings.

available.description: Whether the assistant should be enabled

get.description: Retrieves the current SMTP mail server credentials configured in the system. Requires admin role.

post.description: Tests the SMTP connection with the provided credentials. Requires admin role.
Saves the SMTP mail server credentials to the system configuration. Requires admin role.
Immediately sends a dashboard report via email. Requires admin role.

dashboardId.description: Dashboard ID to include in the report

put.description: Uploads and updates the profile image for the authenticated user.

200.description: Profile image uploaded and saved successfully.

post.description: Uploads a data file (CSV, Excel, GeoJSON, etc.) to be used as a datasource.
Uploads the Google BigQuery service account credentials JSON file. Requires admin role.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: BigQuery service account credentials JSON file

get.description: Reads and returns the content of a GeoJSON file by its datasource ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Datasource ID associated with the GeoJSON file

404.description: GeoJSON file not found for the given ID.

post.description: Runs an ARIMA time-series prediction model on the provided data and returns forecasted values.

steps.description: Number of future steps to forecast

200.description: ARIMA prediction completed, forecasted values returned.

post.description: Runs a TensorFlow/LSTM neural network prediction model on the provided data and returns forecasted values.

steps.description: Number of future steps to forecast

200.description: TensorFlow prediction completed, forecasted values returned.

post.description: Creates a new table in the datasource model with the specified columns and configuration. Requires admin role.

datasourceId.description: ID of the datasource where the table will be created

columns.description: Column definitions for the new table

200.description: Table created successfully in the datasource model.

post.description: Inserts data rows into an existing table of the datasource model. Requires admin role.

datasourceId.description: ID of the datasource containing the target table

tableName.description: Name of the table to insert data into

rows.description: Array of row objects to insert

200.description: Data inserted successfully into the table.

get.description: Retrieves a custom HTML panel content by its unique key identifier.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Unique key identifying the custom HTML panel

404.description: No custom HTML found for the given key.

put.description: Creates or updates a custom HTML panel content identified by the given key. Requires authentication.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Unique key identifying the custom HTML panel

css.description: Optional CSS styles for the panel

200.description: Custom HTML panel created or updated successfully.

post.description: Validates and tests a custom action URL endpoint to verify it is reachable and returns a valid response. Requires admin role.

url.description: The URL of the custom action endpoint to test

method.description: HTTP method to use for the test (GET, POST, etc.)

headers.description: Optional HTTP headers to include in the test request

200.description: Custom action URL is reachable and returned a valid response.

400.description: Custom action URL is not reachable or returned an error.

500.description: Server error testing the custom action URL.

post.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server endpoint. Handles MCP protocol requests including tool calls and resource access via Streamable HTTP transport.

parameters.name: x-user-token
parameters.description: Optional JWT user token to associate the MCP request with an authenticated EDA user

method.description: MCP protocol method name (e.g. tools/call, tools/list)

get.description: Returns the MCP service status and version information.

200.description: MCP service info returned (service name, version, status, transport type).

post.description: AI chat assistant endpoint. Accepts a conversation history and uses an LLM with MCP tools to answer questions about EDA dashboards and data. Requires authentication.

200.description: AI response returned. May include optional dashboard/panel options array for exploration results.

503.description: AI assistant is not available (API key not configured).

get.description: Returns the availability status of the AI chat assistant. Requires authentication.

200.description: Returns object with available boolean indicating if the AI chat is enabled.

get.description: Returns the active login types configured in the system (e.g. local, SAML, Google, Microsoft). Used by the frontend to display the appropriate login buttons.

200.description: Active login type configuration returned successfully.

500.description: Server error retrieving login type configuration.

post.description: Authenticates a user via Google Single Sign-On (SSO). Validates the Google ID token and returns an EDA JWT session token.

token.description: Google OAuth2 ID token obtained from the Google Sign-In flow

200.description: Google SSO authentication successful, EDA JWT token returned.

400.description: Invalid Google token or user not authorized in EDA.

500.description: Server error during Google SSO authentication.

post.description: Authenticates a user via Microsoft Azure AD Single Sign-On (SSO). Validates the Microsoft access token and returns an EDA JWT session token.

token.description: Microsoft Azure AD access token obtained from the MSAL authentication flow

200.description: Microsoft SSO authentication successful, EDA JWT token returned.

400.description: Invalid Microsoft token or user not authorized in EDA.

500.description: Server error during Microsoft SSO authentication.

get.description: Initiates the SAML SSO login flow by redirecting the user to the configured Identity Provider (IdP) login page.

302.description: Redirect to the SAML Identity Provider login URL.

post.description: Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) endpoint. Receives and processes the SAML assertion from the Identity Provider after successful authentication.

parameters.name: SAMLResponse
parameters.description: Base64-encoded SAML response from the Identity Provider

302.description: Redirect to the EDA application with the session token upon successful SAML assertion.

get.description: Returns the SAML Service Provider (SP) metadata XML. Used by the Identity Provider to configure the trust relationship.

200.description: SAML SP metadata XML returned successfully.

get.description: Initiates the SAML Single Logout (SLO) flow by sending a logout request to the Identity Provider. Requires authentication.

302.description: Redirect to the Identity Provider logout URL.

get.description: Receives and processes the SAML logout response or request from the Identity Provider (GET binding).

302.description: Redirect to the EDA application login page after successful logout.

post.description: Receives and processes the SAML logout response or request from the Identity Provider (POST binding).

parameters.name: SAMLResponse
parameters.description: Base64-encoded SAML logout response from the Identity Provider

302.description: Redirect to the EDA application login page after successful logout.
